summary: No blocking security findings for the Phase 0B release-smoke closure.
acceptance_notes: The release smoke blocks global fetch and asserts zero network attempts for both local admin/Radar Task helper coverage and the scan/opportunity/MVP Spec flow.
The local admin path verifies placeholder fail-closed behavior, signed session validation, tamper rejection, protected admin paths, admin-checked Radar Task mutations, and mock-only scan payloads.

README and .env.example do not include real secrets and keep production auth, public deployment, paid providers, real accounts, billing, telemetry, cron, email, and commercial release decisions human-gated.
residual_risks: Release smoke samples route protection; the broader auth test suite carries the full protected-route matrix.
